pullRequest.title: Fix AttributeError when handling 302 redirects: initialize _ws_blacklist as set()
pullRequest.body:
`_ws_blacklist` was typed as `Set[Tuple[int, bool]]` but initialized with `{}` (a `dict`), causing `AttributeError: 'dict' object has no attribute 'add'` on every 302 redirect.

## Change

```python
# Before
_ws_blacklist: Set[Tuple[int, bool]] = {}

# After
_ws_blacklist: Set[Tuple[int, bool]] = set()
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Ошибка AttributeError: 'dict' object has no attribute 'add' при обработке редиректов (302)</issue_title>
> <issue_description>Переменная _ws_blacklist инициализируется как пустой словарь {}, но в коде она используется как множество set через вызов метода .add().
> {} - это пустой словарь, поэтому при попытке добавить в него DC после редиректа возникает исключение:
> AttributeError: 'dict' object has no attribute 'add'
> 
> ```
> 2026-03-04 23:25:18  INFO   tg-ws-proxy  [127.0.0.1:58675] DC203 (91.105.192.100:443) -> wss://kws203-1.telegram.org/apiws via 149.154.167.220
> 
> 2026-03-04 23:25:18  WARNING  tg-ws-proxy  [127.0.0.1:58675] DC203 got 302 from kws203-1.telegram.org -> https://core.telegram.org
> 
> 2026-03-04 23:25:18  ERROR  tg-ws-proxy  [127.0.0.1:58675] unexpected: 'dict' object has no attribute 'add'
> ```
> 
> решение - поменять `_ws_blacklist: Set[Tuple[int, bool]] = {}` на `_ws_blacklist: Set[Tuple[int, bool]] = set()`</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

- Fixes Flowseal/tg-ws-proxy#6
